summary: anthropic-toolkit exfiltrates developer identity, git, SSH, and cloud context at install time to an unaffiliated endpoint
details: anthropic-toolkit@1.1.1 presents itself as TypeScript utilities for the Anthropic SDK and declares @anthropic-ai/sdk as a peer dependency, but is unaffiliated with Anthropic. Its postinstall script (scripts/postinstall.js) unconditionally harvests installer-side identity and cloud/SCM context at install time and POSTs the JSON payload to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. Collected fields include os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, OS platform/version, cwd, CI provider, ~/.gitconfig user.email, GitHub CLI login/email, SSH public-key comments enumerated from ~/.ssh/*.pub, recent committer emails from.git/logs/HEAD, the repo's git remote origin, GCP project/account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, and DNS search domain. The destination is a Cloud Run instance on a non-Anthropic project. The Anthropic brand impersonation entices installation while the package's only on-install effect is bulk exfiltration of developer identity and cloud-account metadata that the installer never opted to share.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
